skill.name: geo-fundamentals
skill.description: Guides Generative Engine Optimization for AI search engines and audits public web pages for citation readiness.
skill.summary: Analyze and improve web content for AI search citation readiness.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Manual review found that the static findings are false positives. The weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:64 are ordinary documentation text, and the duplicate external command alerts at SKILL.md:155 are a documented script invocation, not shell or Ruby backtick execution. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, network exfiltration, or destructive behavior was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alert Is Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:64. These lines are the skill description and a checklist item about step-by-step guides, not cryptographic code or a recommendation to use weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain Markdown and YAML frontmatter. There is no hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection logic at either location.

low_findings.title: External Command Alert Is a Documented Usage Example
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged duplicate Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md:155. The line documents how a user may run the included GEO checker script and does not execute a command inside the skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside a Markdown table as inline code. It is not executable Ruby, shell interpolation, or an instruction that processes untrusted input through a shell.

content.value_statement: AI search engines cite content that is clear, structured, and easy to verify. This skill helps teams assess pages and improve citation readiness for Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and related engines.
actual_capabilities: Explains core Generative Engine Optimization concepts and citation factors.
Compares GEO priorities with traditional SEO priorities.
Lists content elements that improve AI citation readiness.
Identifies crawler access considerations for major AI engines.
Provides a local page audit workflow for HTML, JSX, and TSX pages.
Scores public-facing pages against structured content and attribution signals.
limitations: It cannot guarantee citations from any AI search engine.
The audit script checks page files and does not evaluate live search results.
The guidance is general and may need adaptation for regulated industries.
Markdown documentation is not treated as public web content by the checker.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Content Brief
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to define headings, source citations, author details, and FAQ coverage before drafting.

use_cases.title: Audit a Web Project
use_cases.description: Review public page files for structured data, publication dates, author attribution, and citation-friendly sections.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Visibility Reports
use_cases.description: Add AI citation readiness findings to existing SEO reports without replacing ranking or traffic analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Start a GEO Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use geo-fundamentals to review my public pages for AI citation readiness. Summarize the main gaps in simple terms.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for a site owner who needs a clear starting point.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Draft Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this article draft against the GEO checklist. Focus on definitions, sources, author attribution, dates, and FAQ coverage.
prompt_templates.scenario: Content review before publishing a new article.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Technical Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my page templates for structured data and crawler access issues. Rank fixes by likely impact on AI citation readiness.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer review for page templates or a content platform.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Advanced GEO Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GEO improvement plan for a content library. Include entity building, measurement, crawler policy, and page-level audit priorities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for teams managing many public pages.

output_examples.input: Review my product guide for AI citation readiness.
output_examples.output: The guide has clear headings and useful comparison content. It needs named sources, an author profile, a visible update date, and a short FAQ section.

output_examples.input: Audit my web project and explain the main GEO gaps.
output: Several pages have a single clear H1 and useful lists.
Most pages are missing JSON-LD structured data.
Author attribution and publication dates should be added to article templates.

output_examples.input: Create a GEO plan for my blog archive.
output_examples.output: Start with high-traffic articles, add direct definitions, refresh outdated dates, add FAQ coverage, and document crawler access policy.

best_practices: Add named sources, author credentials, and update dates to important pages.
Use structured headings, direct definitions, tables, lists, and FAQ sections.
Review crawler access decisions before expecting AI search engines to cite content.
anti_patterns: Publishing thin pages that lack sources, dates, or clear authorship.
Treating GEO scores as guaranteed citation outcomes.
Blocking AI crawlers while expecting those engines to discover and cite pages.

faq.question: What is Generative Engine Optimization?
faq.answer: Generative Engine Optimization is the practice of making content easier for AI search engines to understand, retrieve, and cite.

faq.question: Which platforms does this skill discuss?
faq.answer: It discusses AI search behavior for ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini, and related systems.

faq.question: Does the skill change website files?
faq.answer: No. The included checker is intended to inspect page files and report findings.

faq.question: What page types does the checker focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on public-facing HTML, JSX, and TSX files that look like web pages.

faq.question: Can this replace SEO work?
faq.answer: No. It complements SEO by focusing on citation readiness, entity clarity, and answer-friendly structure.

faq.question: Can this guarantee AI citations?
faq.answer: No. AI engines control retrieval and citation decisions, so this skill can only improve readiness.
